records.ety: [F. assortir; <?/ (L. ad) + sortir to cast or draw lots, to obtain by lot, L. sortiri, fr. sors, sortis, lot. See Sort.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Assorted; p. pr. & vb. n. Assorting.]

defs: To separate and distribute into classes, as things of a like kind, nature, or quality, or which are suited to a like purpose; to classify; as, to assort goods. [Rarely applied to persons.]
quotes: They appear . . . no ways assorted to those with whom they must associate.

defs: To furnish with, or make up of, various sorts or a variety of goods; as, to assort a cargo.
To agree; to be in accordance; to be adapted; to suit; to fall into a class or place.
